target.name: Lightelligence
target.founder: Shen Yichen, Marin Soljačić, Huaiyu Meng, Spencer Powers

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lightelligence is a public-stage chips and semiconductors company founded by Shen Yichen, Marin Soljaćić, Huaiyu Meng, and Spencer Powers, having raised $270M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage public peers: Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 3 public comparables is $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. This method directly anchors the analysis given the alignment of stage and sector. A revenue-multiple approach using public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) is not feasible; while the sector P/S median is 22.3x across those three tickers, Lightelligence's revenue is not on file, preventing application of this method.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, centered at $1.5B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than methodological convergence. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three peer comparables—particularly the $5.0B Credo outlier—relative to Lightelligence's technology differentiation and market position. Revenue visibility and path to profitability remain critical but unavailable variables that would sharpen the analysis if disclosed.
